title: Document TUI Refresh And Interaction Behavior
description: Update the README TUI section to describe the manual-first refresh model, explicit refresh-mode controls, focus and visual cues, keyboard and mouse interaction behavior, and any control changes introduced by the runtime update.
acceptance_criteria: README explains that the TUI starts in manual refresh mode and how operators enable timed or continuous refresh behavior.
README documents keyboard and mouse controls for list/detail focus, scrolling, refresh, and operator action flows.
README describes visual or focus cues and expected interaction behavior at a level sufficient for operators to use the updated TUI.
Documentation remains aligned with the shipped runtime behavior and avoids describing out-of-scope redesign work.

handoff_summary.completed: Expanded the TUI documentation in README.md to explain that the screen starts in manual refresh mode, clarified how `a` and `S` control timed refresh versus timed scheduler runs, documented list/detail focus cues, and separated keyboard and mouse interaction guidance for operators.
handoff_summary.remaining: No documentation gaps found within this bead's scope. No runtime or test changes were made.
handoff_summary.risks: Docs are aligned to current TUI behavior in src/codex_orchestrator/tui.py and tests/test_tui.py. If B0062 changes shortcuts, mode text, or mouse/focus behavior again, README.md will need a follow-up sync.
handoff_summary.next_action: Proceed with downstream review or integration using the updated README wording for TUI operator behavior.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: README.md TUI section was updated in place. Downstream agents editing the same section should rebase carefully to avoid overwriting the new refresh-mode, focus, and mouse-behavior wording.

conflict_risks: README.md TUI section was updated in place. Downstream agents editing the same section should rebase carefully to avoid overwriting the new refresh-mode, focus, and mouse-behavior wording.

execution_history.summary: Updated the README TUI section to document the manual-first refresh model, explicit refresh-mode controls, focus cues, and keyboard and mouse interaction behavior in line with the shipped runtime.

guardrails.template_text:
# Documentation Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Update documentation and examples that explain the assigned behavior without changing runtime feature behavior.

Allowed actions:
- Edit docs, examples, and explanatory text tied to the assigned bead.
- Align documentation with existing code and validated behavior.
- Identify when implementation or tests must land first and block with a handoff.

Disallowed actions:
- Change runtime behavior, production code paths, or feature logic.
- Invent undocumented behavior that is not present in the codebase.
- Approve code quality or test completeness as a substitute for review.

Expected outputs:
- Completed or blocked JSON with concise documentation changes and remaining gaps.
- Accurate updated docs and clear next-agent recommendations when code changes are required first.
